sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed - compare to previous frame
if previous_frame:
    prev_seg = previous_frame.segmentation
    curr_seg = current_frame.segmentation
    prev_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in prev_seg['nodes']}
    curr_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in curr_seg['nodes']}
    for nid in set(list(prev_nodes.keys()) + list(curr_nodes.keys())):
        pn = prev_nodes.get(nid)
        cn = curr_nodes.get(nid)
        if pn is None:
            print(f"Node {nid}: NEW, color={cn['color']}, pixels={cn['pixels']}")
        elif cn is None:
            print(f"Node {nid}: REMOVED, color={pn['color']}, pixels={pn['pixels']}")
        elif pn['color'] != cn['color'] or pn['pixels'] != cn['pixels'] or pn['boundary'] != cn['boundary']:
            print(f"Node {nid}: CHANGED, color={pn['color']}->{cn['color']}, pixels={pn['pixels']}->{cn['pixels']}, boundary={pn['boundary']}->{cn['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The only changes are in the HUD/timer bar at the bottom (node 10 and node 14). The gameplay objects didn't change at all. So clicking on the goal panel block just changed the timer, not the game state.
Let me step back and think about this problem more carefully. I've been going in circles with SPACE. The only thing that changes the canvas is SPACE, and SPACE always fills from the top.
Current state: bucket=white(84px), canvas=white(100px)
Goal: white on top, purple on bottom (matching the goal panel)
What if I need to:
1. Change bucket to purple
2. SPACE → canvas becomes purple(50px)+white(50px)
3. Change bucket to white
4. SPACE → canvas becomes white(100px) (purple block becomes white, merges with white block)
5. Change bucket to purple
6. SPACE → canvas becomes purple(50px)+white(50px)
This is the loop I've been stuck in.
Wait, what if the goal is actually purple on top and white on bottom? Let me re-examine the goal panel. The goal panel shows:
- Node 5: white (50px), boundary [[3, 3], [3, 12], [7, 12], [7, 3]]
- Node 9: purple (50px), boundary [[8, 3], [8, 12], [12, 12], [12, 3]]
So the goal panel has white on top (row 3-7) and purple on bottom (row 8-12). The canvas currently has white(100px) which is one block.
What if the game is actually already solved when the canvas is a single white block? But the game hasn't completed...
Let me try a completely different approach. What if I need to click on the gray frame (node 11) or the bucket itself to do something?
Or what if the game requires me to use the arrow keys to move the bucket left/right/up/down?
Let me try pressing LEFT or RIGHT to see if the bucket moves.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try moving the bucket with arrow keys
action(['LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
